verses.commentary: By the command of God establishes apostleship as divine decree, not human ambition. God our Savior and Christ Jesus our hope apply titles distinctively: Father as Savior, Son as hope, each revealing different aspects of salvation.

My true son in the faith indicates genuine spiritual lineage. Grace, mercy and peace adds mercy to the usual grace and peace, perhaps reflecting Timothy's particular need for compassion amid difficult ministry.

As I urged you when I went into Macedonia, stay there in Ephesus recalls prior instruction. Command certain people not to teach false doctrines establishes Timothy's authority to silence error. Staying means enduring, not fleeing difficulty.

Or to devote themselves to myths and endless genealogies identifies the false teaching: speculative fiction. Promote controversial speculations rather than advancing God's work shows fruitless results. Which is by faith contrasts: God's work advances through faith, not speculation.

The goal of this command is love exposes the telos: right teaching produces love. Which comes from a pure heart and a good conscience and a sincere faith traces love's sources. Orthodoxy serves orthopraxy; doctrine aims at devotion.

Some have departed from these and have turned to meaningless talk describes apostasy's trajectory. Departure from heart-faith-conscience leads to empty words. Loss of center produces peripheral babble.

They want to be teachers of the law reveals their aspiration. But they do not know what they are talking about exposes ignorance beneath confidence. What they so confidently affirm intensifies the irony: certainty without comprehension.
We know that the law is good if one uses it properly affirms law's value while qualifying its use. Misuse of good thing produces harm. Proper use requires understanding the law's purpose.

The law is made not for the righteous but for lawbreakers establishes law's function: restraining evil, not justifying good. The catalog of sins that follows illustrates those needing legal restraint.

The vice list expands to include sexual immorality, homosexuality, slave traders, liars, and perjurers. And for whatever else is contrary to the sound doctrine catches all remaining violations. Sound doctrine provides ethical standard.

That conforms to the gospel concerning the glory of the blessed God links ethics to gospel. Which he entrusted to me grounds Paul's authority in divine commission. Gospel entrusted creates stewardship obligation.

I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who has given me strength shifts to testimony. That he considered me trustworthy, appointing me to his service marvels at divine trust despite unworthiness. Strength and trustworthiness come from Christ.

Even though I was once a blasphemer and a persecutor and a violent man rehearses former identity. I was shown mercy because I acted in ignorance and unbelief explains ground of mercy: sin from ignorance, not willful rebellion, though still culpable.

The grace of our Lord was poured out on me abundantly describes lavish grace. Along with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus shows grace brings its gifts. Grace produces faith and love as its fruit.

Here is a trustworthy saying introduces a creedal formula. Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners states gospel in summary. Of whom I am the worst makes Paul's testimony personal and present tense: not was but am, reflecting ongoing awareness of sin's gravity.

But for that very reason I was shown mercy so that in me, the worst of sinners, Christ Jesus might display his immense patience reveals purpose: Paul as exhibit of grace. As an example for those who would believe makes his conversion pattern. If Paul can be saved, anyone can.

Now to the King eternal, immortal, invisible, the only God erupts in doxology. Four divine attributes: eternal (timeless), immortal (deathless), invisible (incorporeal), only (unique). Be honor and glory for ever and ever. Amen ascribes perpetual worship.

Timothy, my son personalizes the charge. I am giving you this command in keeping with the prophecies once made about you recalls prophetic words over Timothy. So that by recalling them you may fight the battle well connects prophecy to perseverance. Remember your calling when the fight gets hard.

Holding on to faith and a good conscience lists two essentials. Which some have rejected and so have suffered shipwreck with regard to the faith shows consequence: reject conscience, lose faith. Conscience and doctrine are inseparable.

Among them are Hymenaeus and Alexander names specific apostates. Whom I have handed over to Satan to be taught not to blaspheme describes excommunication for discipline. Delivered to Satan's realm outside church protection for corrective, not merely punitive, purpose.
